system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# Fun4Me Store — Product Roadmap v4.0
## Post-Audit Edition — Gaps Closed, Stories Complete

*Upgraded from v3.0 after full codebase + live site audit on [REDACTED_PHONE]*

---

## CURRENT STATE SUMMARY

### What's Live
- Age verification gate (DOB + 30-day cookie)
- Full storefront: homepage, categories (8), kinks (6), product detail, search
- 54 sample products in Supabase (12 tables)
- Cart drawer + cart page with free shipping bar
- Checkout flow (shipping form, zone detection, bank transfer / COD)
- Admin panel (dashboard, product CRUD, order management)
- SEO (sitemap.xml, robots.txt, JSON-LD, Open Graph)
- Ofertas (deals) page
- WhatsApp floating button
- Deployed on Hostinger VPS via Docker + Traefik

### What's Broken (Critical Bugs Found in Audit)
| # | Bug | Severity |
|---|-----|----------|
| B1 | Checkout does NOT save orders to Supabase — only sessionStorage | CRITICAL |
| B2 | Admin panel has ZERO auth protection — anyone can access /admin | CRITICAL |
| B3 | Product images don't show in cart (image_url vs images[] mismatch) | HIGH |
| B4 | Free shipping threshold inconsistent (300K vs 500K) | MEDIUM |
| B5 | Login page is non-functional stub (no Supabase auth) | HIGH |
| B6 | Newsletter signup does nothing (no API call) | MEDIUM |
| B7 | Footer "Ayuda" links are dead (Envios, Devolucion, FAQ, Privacidad) | MEDIUM |
| B8 | Ofertas nav link points to /#ofertas instead of /ofertas | LOW |
| B9 | Cart hydration mismatch (no mounted guard in drawer/cart page) | MEDIUM |
| B10 | Nested interactive elements: button inside Link in ProductCard | MEDIUM |
| B11 | formatPrice duplicated in 3 admin files | LOW |
| B12 | updateQuantity doesn't check max_stock | LOW |
| B13 | WhatsApp number masked in constants.ts but shown in footer | LOW |

---

## PHASE 1.5: BUG FIXES + HARDENING (Before anything else)

> **Goal:** Make the existing site actually work end-to-end.
> **Timeline:** 1-2 days. No new features until these are done.
> **Can do without client input:** YES — all items.

### EPIC: Critical Bug Fixes
| # | Story | Hrs | Priority |
|---|-------|-----|----------|
| H1 | Checkout: create API route that writes orders to Supabase (orders + order_items tables) | 4 | P0 |
| H2 | Checkout: validate stock before placing order, return error if insufficient | 2 | P0 |
| H3 | Checkout: add email field to form (needed for receipts + future comms) | 1 | P0 |
| H4 | Checkout: upload bank transfer receipt to Supabase Storage | 2 | P0 |
| H5 | Checkout: generate real order number (sequential via DB) not timestamp | 1 | P0 |
| H6 | Confirmation page: fetch order from Supabase by ID (don't rely on sessionStorage) | 2 | P0 |

### EPIC: Security Hardening
| # | Story | Hrs | Priority |
|---|-------|-----|----------|
| H7 | Admin auth: implement Supabase auth check on /admin routes via middleware | 3 | P0 |
| H8 | Login page: wire up Supabase email/password auth with error handling | 2 | P0 |
| H9 | Admin RLS: ensure products/orders writes require admin role (not anonymous) | 2 | P0 |
| H10 | Add rate limiting to checkout API route (prevent spam orders) | 1 | P1 |

### EPIC: Data Integrity Fixes
| # | Story | Hrs | Priority |
|---|-------|-----|----------|
| H11 | Fix ProductCard: read images[0] instead of image_url for product photos | 1 | P1 |
| H12 | Fix cart store: pass image_url when adding items so cart shows product images | 1 | P1 |
| H13 | Normalize free shipping threshold to single constant (300,000 Gs) | 0.5 | P1 |
| H14 | Fix Ofertas nav link: point to /ofertas not /#ofertas | 0.5 | P1 |
| H15 | Fix cart hydration: add mounted state guard in cart-drawer and carrito page | 1 | P1 |
| H16 | Fix ProductCard: move "Add to Cart" button outside the Link element | 1 | P1 |
| H17 | Consolidate formatPrice — use shared lib/utils/format.ts everywhere | 1 | P1 |
| H18 | Fix updateQuantity to check max_stock | 0.5 | P2 |
| H19 | Fix WhatsApp number consistency across constants.ts and footer | 0.5 | P2 |

### EPIC: Error Handling + Loading States
| # | Story | Hrs | Priority |
|---|-------|-----|----------|
| H20 | Add error.tsx boundary at (store) layout level (Spanish error page) | 1 | P1 |
| H21 | Add not-found.tsx at (store) layout level (Spanish 404 page) | 1 | P1 |
| H22 | Add loading.tsx skeletons: homepage, category, product detail, search, admin | 5 | P1 |
| H23 | Add error handling to all Supabase queries (graceful fallback, not blank page) | 2 | P1 |
| H24 | Header: add loading state while categories fetch client-side | 1 | P2 |

### EPIC: Accessibility
| # | Story | Hrs | Priority |
|---|-------|-----|----------|
| H25 | Add aria-labels to cart quantity buttons (+/-/delete) | 0.5 | P2 |
| H26 | Add aria-pressed to filter/zone/payment selection buttons | 1 | P2 |
| H27 | Add role="tab"/aria-selected to ProductTabs | 1 | P2 |
| H28 | Add aria-expanded to mobile menu hamburger + categories dropdown | 0.5 | P2 |
| H29 | Add role="progressbar" + aria-valuenow to free shipping bar | 0.5 | P2 |

**Phase 1.5 total: ~38h | EXIT: Site actually processes orders. Admin is protected.**

{"output": "-- =============================================================================
-- Fun4Me Store - Phase 1 Initial Schema
-- Paraguayan Adult Store / Sex Shop E-commerce Platform
-- All prices in Guaranies (PYG) as bigint (no decimals)
-- =============================================================================

-- Enable required extensions
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pgcrypto";

-- =============================================================================
-- 1. CATEGORIES
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE categories (
  id         UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  name       TEXT NOT NULL,
  slug       TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  description TEXT,
  parent_id  UUID REFERENCES categories(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  image_url  TEXT,
  sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  is_active  BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_categories_parent ON categories(parent_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_categories_slug ON categories(slug);
CREATE INDEX idx_categories_active ON categories(is_active) WHERE is_active = true;

-- =============================================================================
-- 2. PRODUCTS
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE products (
  id                UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  name              TEXT NOT NULL,
  slug              TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  description       TEXT,
  price             BIGINT NOT NULL CHECK (price >= 0),
  compare_at_price  BIGINT CHECK (compare_at_price IS NULL OR compare_at_price >= 0),
  category_id       UUID REFERENCES categories(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  brand             TEXT,
  sku               TEXT UNIQUE,
  images            TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  tags              TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  experience_level  TEXT CHECK (experience_level IN ('beginner', 'intermediate', 'advanced')),
  material          TEXT,
  is_active         BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  is_featured       BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  stock_quantity    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  meta_title        TEXT,
  meta_description  TEXT,
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_products_category ON products(category_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_slug ON products(slug);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_active ON products(is_active) WHERE is_active = true;
CREATE INDEX idx_products_featured ON products(is_featured) WHERE is_featured = true;
CREATE INDEX idx_products_sku ON products(sku);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_tags ON products USING GIN(tags);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_price ON products(price);

-- Auto-update updated_at trigger
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  NEW.updated_at = now();
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER trg_products_updated_at
  BEFORE UPDATE ON products
  FOR EACH ROW
  EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column();

-- =============================================================================
-- 3. KINK CATEGORIES
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE kink_categories (
  id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  name        TEXT NOT NULL,
  slug        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  description TEXT,
  image_url   TEXT,
  sort_order  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);

CREATE INDEX idx_kink_categories_slug ON kink_categories(slug);

-- =============================================================================
-- 4. PRODUCT_KINKS (junction table)
-- =============================================================================
CREATE TABLE product_kinks (
  product_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  kink_id    UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES kink_categories(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  PRIMARY KEY (product_id, kink_id)
);

## PHASE 1: USER ACCOUNT SYSTEM (12h)

### 1.1 Supabase Schema — User Profiles (1h)

Extend the existing `customers` table (already linked to `auth.users`). New migration `002_user_profiles.sql`:

```sql

ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS avatar_url TEXT;
```

### 1.2 User Auth Pages (3h)

- **Signup page** — `/registro` — email + password + name + phone + DOB. Auto-create `customers` row on signup via Supabase `on_auth_user_created` trigger
- **Login page** — `/iniciar-sesion` — already exists at `/(auth)/login/page.tsx`. Enhance with redirect support, "forgot password" link
- **Forgot password** — `/recuperar-contrasena`
- **Account page** — `/cuenta` — tabbed layout:
  - **Profile** — edit name, email, phone, DOB, avatar
  - **Addresses** — CRUD multiple addresses with "set as default"
  - **Orders** — order history with status tracking
  - **Preferences** — newsletter, notifications toggle
  - **CI/ID** — upload and manage identification document

### 1.3 User Menu in Header (1h)

- Show login/register when not authenticated
- Show user avatar + dropdown (Mi Cuenta, Mis Pedidos, Cerrar Sesión) when authenticated
- Persist session via Supabase cookies

### 1.4 Checkout with User Data (3h)

- If user is logged in: auto-fill name, email, phone, saved addresses
- Guest checkout: still works, but with prompt: "Creá una cuenta para guardar tus datos"
- After purchase: option to save address to account
- Logged-in users get order confirmation linked to their account
- Guest checkout auto-creates account if they provide email (optional)

### 1.5 Trigger: Auto-create customer profile (1h)

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION handle_new_user()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.customers (id, full_name, email, phone)
  VALUES (
    NEW.id,
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'name', ''),
    NEW.email,
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'phone', '')
  );
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created
  AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION handle_new_user();
```

### 1.6 Frontend Auth Provider (2h)

- Create `AuthContext` that wraps the app
- Provides: `user`, `profile`, `signIn`, `signUp`, `signOut`, `updateProfile`
- All authenticated pages use this context

### 1.7 Profile API Routes (1h)

- `GET /api/profile` — fetch current user + profile
- `PUT /api/profile` — update profile fields
- `GET /api/addresses` — list addresses
- `POST /api/addresses` — create address
- `PUT /api/addresses/[id]` — update address
- `DELETE /api/addresses/[id]` — delete address

### 2.2 CI Upload Flow (3h)

**In user profile /cuenta section:**
- Upload CI image (front + back) with crop preview
- Enter CI number manually (text field)

- Submit for admin verification
- Status badge: "No verificada" (gray) → "Pendiente" (yellow) → "Verificada" (green) → "Rechazada" (red)

### 2.3 CI Verification Admin Panel (3h)

**New admin page: `/admin/verificaciones`**
- List of all CI submissions sorted by date
- Each item shows: CI image thumbnail, CI number, full name, customer info

- Search by CI number, customer name, status
- Sort by date, status

### 2.4 CI in Checkout (2h)

**For ticket purchases (REQUIRED):**
- Checkout enforces CI upload + verification before proceeding
- If user has verified CI on file → auto-attach to order
- If not → show upload form inline, must complete before payment
- Block purchase if CI is blacklisted

**For toy purchases (OPTIONAL):**
- Checkout offers CI as optional field
- If provided, attach to order for age verification record

### 2.5 CI Blacklist Check (1h)

- Before order submission: check CI number against `blacklist` table
- If blacklisted: show error "No se puede procesar tu pedido. Contactanos para más información."
- Block both toys and tickets if CI is blacklisted

### 3.2 Event Pages (3h)

# Plan Mode

Use this skill when the user wants a plan instead of execution.

## Core behavior

For this turn, you are planning only.

- Do not implement code.
- Do not edit project files except the plan markdown file.
- Do not run mutating terminal commands, commit, push, or perform external actions.
- You may inspect the repo or other context with read-only commands/tools when needed.
- Your deliverable is a markdown plan saved inside the active workspace under `.hermes/plans/`.

## Output requirements

Write a markdown plan that is concrete and actionable.

Include, when relevant:
- Goal
- Current context / assumptions
- Proposed approach
- Step-by-step plan
- Files likely to change
- Tests / validation
- Risks, tradeoffs, and open questions

If the task is code-related, include exact file paths, likely test targets, and verification steps.

## Save location

Save the plan with `write_file` under:
- `.hermes/plans/YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSS-<slug>.md`

Treat that as relative to the active working directory / backend workspace. Hermes file tools are backend-aware, so using this relative path keeps the plan with the workspace on local, docker, ssh, modal, and daytona backends.

If the runtime provides a specific target path, use that exact path.
If not, create a sensible timestamped filename yourself under `.hermes/plans/`.

## Interaction style

- If the request is clear enough, write the plan directly.
- If no explicit instruction accompanies `/plan`, infer the task from the current conversation context.
- If it is genuinely underspecified, ask a brief clarifying question instead of guessing.
- After saving the plan, reply briefly with what you planned and the saved path.

# Writing Implementation Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the implementer has zero context for the codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need: which files to touch, complete code, testing commands, docs to check, how to verify. Give them bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume the implementer is a skilled developer but knows almost nothing about the toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Core principle:** A good plan makes implementation obvious. If someone has to guess, the plan is incomplete.

## When to Use

**Always use before:**
- Implementing multi-step features
- Breaking down complex requirements
- Delegating to subagents via subagent-driven-development
- **Auditing an existing codebase** — skip the TDD cycle, use the audit checklist format instead

## Plan Types

### Type A: Implementation Plan (build something new)
Bite-sized tasks with TDD cycles, code examples, file paths, test commands. Used for features, fixes, refactoring.

### Type B: Audit Plan (analyze existing code)
When a user says "analyze everything" or asks for a comprehensive todo list, write a **categorized audit** instead of an implementation plan.

**Audit format:**
```
# [Project Name] — Full Audit (N Items)

> Deep analysis, critique, and actionable todo list.
> Generated: DATE

## Summary Table

| Area | Items | Status | Biggest Issue |
|------|-------|--------|---------------|
| DATA | 100 | 🟡 | Placeholder WhatsApp |
| SEO | 100 | 🔴 | No structured data |
| ... | ... | ... | ... |

---

## Section 1: Data & Config (Items 000-099)

- [ ] 001 Task description
- [ ] 002 Task description
...
```

**Audit structure rules:**
1. **100 items per major area** — DATA, SEO, UX, CONTENT, ECOMMERCE, IMAGES, CODE (700 total)
2. **50 items per minor area** — MOBILE, PERF, SECURITY, SEO_TECH, DOMAIN, LAUNCH (300 total)
3. **Each item is one actionable checkbox** — not a discussion, not an explanation. One sentence.
4. **Items are numbered 000-999** for reference across documents
5. **Status indicators:** 🟢 done, 🟡 partial, 🔴 critical, ⚪ unknown
6. **Save each area as a separate file** under `docs/audit/{number}-{area}.md`
7. **Create a README.md summary** with: item counts, quick wins (top 20), critical path, status table
8. **Always include a "Critical Path" section** showing the 5-10 items that block launch
9. **Always include a "Quick Wins" table** with effort/impact ratings

**Labels for effort/impact:**
```
| Item | Effort | Impact | Why |
| sitemap URL fix | 2 min | High | Google can't index |
```

**Audit sources to read exhaustively before writing:**
- Every page/component file (`find . -name "*.tsx" -not -path '*/node_modules/*'`)
- Every config file
- Every content file
- `next.config.ts`, `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml`
- `package.json` dependencies
- All docs/ files (check for contradictions with actual code)

**Do NOT skip** the summary file — it's the user's entry point.

**For 1000-item audits specifically:** distribute across 9 files:
- `docs/audit/README.md` — summary, quick wins, critical path
- `docs/audit/000-data.md` — business identity, location, hours, contact, legal (100 items)
- `docs/audit/100-seo.md` — titles, meta, structured data, sitemap, keywords (100 items)
- `docs/audit/200-ux.md` — hero, navigation, cards, grid, promo (100 items)
- `docs/audit/300-content.md` — tone, copy, blog, FAQ, about (100 items)
- `docs/audit/400-ecommerce.md` — cart, payment, products, accounts (100 items)
- `docs/audit/500-images.md` — product photos, logo, icons, optimization (100 items)
- `docs/audit/600-code.md` — TypeScript, components, deps, error handling (100 items)
- `docs/audit/700-launch.md` — mobile, perf, security, domain, launch (300 items)

## Remember

```
Bite-sized tasks (2-5 min each)
Exact file paths
Complete code (copy-pasteable)
Exact commands with expected output
Verification steps
DRY, YAGNI, TDD
Frequent commits
```

or for audits:

```
100 items per major area
50 items per minor area
One actionable sentence per item
Separate file per area
Summary README with quick wins + critical path
Catches contradictions between docs and code
```

**A good plan makes implementation obvious.**

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each task = 2-5 minutes of focused work.**

Every step is one action:
- "Write the failing test" — step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" — step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" — step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" — step
- "Commit" — step

**Too big:**
```markdown
### Task 1: Build authentication system
[50 lines of code across 5 files]
```

**Right size:**
```markdown
### Task 1: Create User model with email field
[10 lines, 1 file]

### Task 2: Add password hash field to User
[8 lines, 1 file]

### Task 3: Create password hashing utility
[15 lines, 1 file]
```

## Plan Document Structure

### Header (Required)

Every plan MUST start with:

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For Hermes:** Use subagent-driven-development skill to implement this plan task-by-task.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

### Task Structure

Each task follows this format:

````markdown
### Task N: [Descriptive Name]

**Objective:** What this task accomplishes (one sentence)

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/new_file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:45-67` (line numbers if known)
- Test: `tests/path/to/test_file.py`

**Step 1: Write failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

**Step 2: Run test to verify failure**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_specific_behavior -v`
Expected: FAIL — "function not defined"

**Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

**Step 4: Run test to verify pass**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_specific_behavior -v`
Expected: PASS

**Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Writing Process

### Step 1: Understand Requirements

Read and understand:
- Feature requirements
- Design documents or user description
- Acceptance criteria
- Constraints

### Step 2: Explore the Codebase

Use Hermes tools to understand the project:

```python
# Understand project structure
search_files("*.py", target="files", path="src/")

# Look at similar features
search_files("similar_pattern", path="src/", file_glob="*.py")

# Check existing tests
search_files("*.py", target="files", path="tests/")

# Read key files
read_file("src/app.py")
```

### Step 3: Design Approach

Decide:
- Architecture pattern
- File organization
- Dependencies needed
- Testing strategy

### Step 4: Write Tasks

Create tasks in order:
1. Setup/infrastructure
2. Core functionality (TDD for each)
3. Edge cases
4. Integration
5. Cleanup/documentation

### Step 5: Add Complete Details

For each task, include:
- **Exact file paths** (not "the config file" but `src/config/settings.py`)
- **Complete code examples** (not "add validation" but the actual code)
- **Exact commands** with expected output
- **Verification steps** that prove the task works

### Step 6: Review the Plan

Check:
- [ ] Tasks are sequential and logical
- [ ] Each task is bite-sized (2-5 min)
- [ ] File paths are exact
- [ ] Code examples are complete (copy-pasteable)
- [ ] Commands are exact with expected output
- [ ] No missing context
- [ ] DRY, YAGNI, TDD principles applied

### Step 7: Save the Plan

```bash
mkdir -p docs/plans
# Save plan to docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-feature-name.md
git add docs/plans/
git commit -m "docs: add implementation plan for [feature]"
```

## Principles

### DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)

**Bad:** Copy-paste validation in 3 places
**Good:** Extract validation function, use everywhere

### YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It)

**Bad:** Add "flexibility" for future requirements
**Good:** Implement only what's needed now

```python
# Bad — YAGNI violation
class User:
    def __init__(self, name, email):
        self.name = name
        self.email = email
        self.preferences = {}  # Not needed yet!
        self.metadata = {}     # Not needed yet!

# Good — YAGNI
class User:
    def __init__(self, name, email):
        self.name = name
        self.email = email
```

### TDD (Test-Driven Development)

Every task that produces code should include the full TDD cycle:
1. Write failing test
2. Run to verify failure
3. Write minimal code
4. Run to verify pass

See `test-driven-development` skill for details.

### Frequent Commits

Commit after every task:
```bash
git add [files]
git commit -m "type: description"
```

## Common Mistakes

### Vague Tasks

**Bad:** "Add authentication"
**Good:** "Create User model with email and password_hash fields"

### Incomplete Code

**Bad:** "Step 1: Add validation function"
**Good:** "Step 1: Add validation function" followed by the complete function code

### Missing Verification

**Bad:** "Step 3: Test it works"
**Good:** "Step 3: Run `pytest tests/test_auth.py -v`, expected: 3 passed"

### Missing File Paths

**Bad:** "Create the model file"
**Good:** "Create: `src/models/user.py`"

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan, offer the execution approach:

**"Plan complete and saved. Ready to execute using subagent-driven-development — I'll dispatch a fresh subagent per task with two-stage review (spec compliance then code quality). Shall I proceed?"**

When executing, use the `subagent-driven-development` skill:
- Fresh `delegate_task` per task with full context
- Spec compliance review after each task
- Code quality review after spec passes
- Proceed only when both reviews approve

## Remember

```
Bite-sized tasks (2-5 min each)
Exact file paths
Complete code (copy-pasteable)
Exact commands with expected output
Verification steps
DRY, YAGNI, TDD
Frequent commits
```

**A good plan makes implementation obvious.**

# Standalone E-commerce Store

Build, audit, and deploy production Next.js 15 e-commerce stores.

## Architecture

Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) -> VPS -> Traefik -> service_web:3000

## Tech Stack

Next.js 15 (standalone output), React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Docker + Swarm, Traefik, SQLite (optional)

## Build & Deploy

```
npm run build
docker build -t <name>:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml <name>
```

## Page Structure

Home, Tienda, Producto/[slug], Envio, FAQ, Contacto, Privacidad, Terminos, Nosotros, Promociones, Blog (posts + categories + [slug]), Sitemap, Error, Not-found, RSS

## Product Data Structure (Retail)

Each product should carry rich spec data, not just name+price:

```json
{
  "slug": "titanium",
  "name": "Titanium",
  "line": "Premium",
  "category": "Resorte",
  "price": "Gs. 1.800.000",
  "priceFrom": "2 plazas 140x190",
  "image": "/images/products/titanium.png",
  "description": "Premium con Euro Pillow Top.",
  "sizes": ["2 plazas 140x190: Gs. 1.800.000", "Queen 160x200: Gs. 2.200.000"],
  "firmness": "Firme",
  "warranty": "5 años",
  "weight": "Hasta 120 kg por lado",
  "colors": ["Gris", "Azul"],
  "badges": ["Pillow Top", "Antiácaros", "Más vendido"]
}
```

Product pages render a specs grid (firmness, warranty, weight, colors), a measure/price table, and badges.

## Retail-Specific Features

- **Bundles/Combos**: Section on homepage showing 2-3 product bundles with original price, bundle price, savings, and WhatsApp inquiry link
- **Promo banners**: Downloadable from client's current site, displayed as clickable 3-column grid in `#0F1624` section
- **Store locator**: GPS coordinates per store, clickable address → Google Maps, clickable phone → tel: link, type badges (Store/Mall/Outlet/Distribución), Argentina subsidiary section
- **Product page**: Specs grid (firmness, warranty, weight, colors), size/price table, badges row
- **Cross-sell**: "Complete your bundle" section — mattress + base + protector + pillows

## Critical Patterns

- WhatsApp-first commerce (all CTAs go to WhatsApp)
- Content-driven from content/es.json
- Age-gated content (localStorage with 7-day timestamp expiry)
- Discreet shipping guarantee (for adult stores)

## Content Structure

content/es.json sections: siteConfig, navigation, home, products (14+ with id/slug/name/price/level/sound/safety fields), categories, faq, blog, footer, shipping, payments, socialProof, brand

## Comprehensive Site Audit Checklist

Run on EVERY page. Be brutal.

### Audit Dimensions:

1. Visual/Design -- spacing, alignment, color, images, fonts, hierarchy, consistency
2. UX/Usability -- navigation, button placement, mobile, loading states, forms, click targets
3. Content -- copy quality, tone, grammar, completeness, placeholders, length
4. E-commerce -- conversion flow, trust signals, pricing, urgency, social proof, stock
5. Technical -- load speed, console errors, broken links, SEO, titles, structured data, a11y
6. Mobile -- viewport, tap targets, readability, nav overlap
7. Industry-specific -- privacy signals, discretion tone, age gate, comfort for first-timers
8. Cross-cutting -- image placeholders, inconsistent icons, duplication, text bugs, keyboard nav

### Severity Triage:

- CRITICAL -- Broken functionality, legal exposure, blank pages. Fix immediately.
- HIGH -- Trust-killing issues (no contact info, no product images, duplicate nav). Fix this batch.
- MEDIUM -- UX friction, redundant elements. Fix alongside HIGH.
- LOW -- Polish, accessibility extras. Fix when time permits.

### Batch Fix Strategy:

1. Fix all CRITICAL first (1-3 line fixes)
2. Fix all HIGH second (users notice these)
3. Fix MEDIUM alongside HIGH
4. Fix LOW last
5. Commit + deploy as single atomic batch

### Overview of this session (Fun4Me full upgrade)

This session built on a basic skeleton store (Fun4Me, adult toys, Paraguay) and transformed it into a full-featured store matching the production El Viajero store. 25+ batches across: content overhaul, 15 pages, full search/filter/sort/pagination, adult-store privacy features (quick exit, privacy mode blur, anonymous reviews), WhatsApp commerce features, carousels, dark/light mode, PWA, structured data, SVG icons, loading states, product comparison, shipping calculator, product quiz, social proof, per-product FAQ, safety badges, sound level indicators, and a complete 520-item feature inventory.

Then ran a brutal full-site roast across all pages and fixed all 21 identified issues in a single follow-up pass.

## Personal Portfolio Sites (Professionals)

When building a portfolio for a **professional individual** (analyst, engineer, consultant, manager — not a band/artist), use a distinct pattern from brand/creative sites.

### Content Pattern
- **Projects/dashboards**: Card grid with title, org, description, tags, and metrics — not product data
- **Skills/proficiency**: Animated skill bars with percentage levels
- **Experience timeline**: Vertical timeline with role, org, dates, and achievement bullets
- **Stats row**: Numerical highlights (markets covered, dashboards built, years experience)
- **Contact**: Simple contact cards (no merch, no events, no newsletter)

### Data Structure
Projects use a dedicated array, not es.json:
```ts
const projects = [
  {
    title: 'Dashboard Name',
    subtitle: 'Org — Department/Region',
    desc: 'What it does and what it achieved',
    tags: ['Power BI', 'DAX', 'SQL'],
    metrics: ['180+ markets', '40% faster'],
    color: 'from-purple-500 to-violet-600'
  }
]
```

Skills as proficiency objects:
```ts
const skills = [
  { name: 'Power BI', level: 95 },
  { name: 'SQL', level: 85 }
]
```

### Mystic Purple Theme (Professional Dark Portfolio)
For a professional dark theme with purple accent:
- Background: `linear-gradient(135deg, #0f0a1a, #1a0f2e, #2e1065, #1a0f2e, #0f0a1a)`
- Cards: Glass-morphism (`rgba(30,15,60,0.6)` + `backdrop-filter: blur(12px)` + purple border glow)
- Text: `#e2d9f0` body, `#c4adff` accent
- Gradients: `linear-gradient(135deg, #c4adff, #8b5cf6, #a880ff)` for headings
- Glow: `box-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(139,92,246,0.3)` on CTAs
- Animation: Subtle float animation on hero (4s ease-in-out)
- SVG chart icon as dashboard placeholder, not product image

See `references/personal-portfolio-pattern.md` for the full component structure and theme CSS.

### Page Structure
- Nav: Fixed top bar with hash-link scroll navigation
- Hero: Title + subtitle + stat row + CTA buttons
- Projects: 1-3 column responsive grid of glass cards with dashboard mockup, tags, metrics
- Skills: 2-column bar chart with percentage labels
- Experience: Left-aligned vertical timeline with glow dots and role cards
- Contact: Centered single-column contact card with platform icons

### Build & Deploy
Same as standalone e-commerce: `next build` → Docker + Traefik, or `output: 'export'` for GitHub Pages.

### Personal Data Stripping for Artist Sites from Personal Repos

When building a public artist/band site from personal content (psychology repos, journals, letters):

**MUST strip before publishing:**
- Real names (family, partners, your own full name)
- Specific medical/health disclosures (HIV, medications, treatment details)
- Family role descriptions ("The Engineer", "child who wanted to be held" → "heart that wanted to be held")
- Place-specific references ("in my own home" → "in the peripheries")
- Specific biographical facts that could identify the person
- Age, location, employer, educational details

**Rewrite strategy:**
- Replace specific disclosures with universal emotional truths
- "I am not a risk / I am not a disease" → "I am not my past / I am not my shame"
- "My family didn't know what to do with me" → "I learned young that feelings were a dangerous thing"
- Keep the emotional core intact while removing identifying specifics

**Checklist:** run `grep -rni 'family|parents|mother|father|hiv|positive|diagnos|weiss|real name'` on all site files before deploy.

### Songs/Lyrics Content Pattern

For artist sites with original music, use a `src/data/songs.ts` data module instead of es.json:

```ts
export const songs = [
  {
    id: 'song-slug',
    title: 'Song Title',
    subtitle: 'One-line hook or tagline',
    lyrics: `Full lyrics as template literal`,
    genre: 'Alternative',
    duration: '4:12',
    mood: 'Vulnerable, building to powerful'
  }
]
```

Then render an **accordion/expandable lyrics section** — clicking a song title expands to show full lyrics beneath it. Use `useState` to track which song is open.

```tsx
// Key pattern for lyrics accordion:
const [openSong, setOpenSong] = useState<string | null>(null)
const toggleSong = (id: string) => setOpenSong(openSong === id ? null : id)

// Each song row is a <button onClick={toggleSong(song.id)}> that toggles a collapsible div
// The div uses animate-in: fade-in slide-in-from-top-2
// Inside the expanded panel: h-px divider + lyrics as whitespace-pre-line text
```

**CRITICAL: All interactive sections (music player, merch shop, events, lyrics accordion, contact) must be `'use client'` components** because they use `useState`, `useEffect`, or `onClick` handlers. Build them as section components in `components/sections/` and import them into the server-side page.

### Page Structure for Band Sites

- **Home**: Hero headline/subheadline, bio section, feature cards (discography, shows, members), Spotify embed iframe, discography cards with album art, team grid (members + role + bio), testimonials, newsletter signup, booking CTA
- **Música**: Full discography with album art per item (from Spotify CDN), track listing per album, Spotify embed
- **Videos**: YouTube embed cards — extract video ID from URL with regex `/watch\?v=([^&]+)/`
- **Letras**: Song title + album metadata, lyrics displayed as lines in a styled dark box
- **Shows**: Event cards with date, venue, description — Calendar icon per card
- **Galería**: Photo grid with category filter buttons (Todas / En Vivo / Banda / Festivales), hover overlay on images
- **Contacto**: Contact cards for each channel (WhatsApp, Email, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, Spotify) — each with icon + label + hover color matching the platform brand

### Interactive Artist Site Sections (Beyond Content-Driven Pages)

For band/artist sites that need revenue and engagement features, build these as section components + data files:

#### Audio Player / Tracklist (`music-section.tsx`)

A tracklist with functional in-browser audio player:

**Two-phase setup:**
1. Generate demo MP3s using Python tone synthesis + ffmpeg (see `references/audio-player-pattern.md` for full code)
2. Wire the `<audio>` player component (same reference)

**Data source:** `src/data/songs.ts` — array of { id, title, subtitle, lyrics, genre, duration, mood }

**Tracklist rows:** Each track is a clickable row: play button (circle with triangle icon), track number, title, subtitle, genre, duration. Clicking expands the player panel below.

**Click interaction:** clicking a track row sets `playingId` state, which conditionally renders an expanded audio player with:
- Song info header (emoji icon, title, genre/duration/mood metadata)
- **Functional HTML5 audio player** (see `references/audio-player-pattern.md` for component code):
  - Waveform progress bar (80 bars, clickable for seeking)
  - Play/pause button
  - Time counter (m:ss / m:ss)
  - Volume slider
  - Hidden `<audio>` element with `preload="metadata"`
- "Read lyrics" link scrolling to `#lyrics` for the active song

**Streaming links:** `src/data/socials.ts` with { name, url, color } — badge-style links with colored dot, rendered above the tracklist.

**Animations:** `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-top-2` on player panel, pulse bars on the mini-player when "playing".

**Audio file placement:** MP3s go in `public/audio/{song-id}.mp3`. The Dockerfile's `COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public` includes them automatically in standalone builds.

**Alternative to real recordings:** When no actual recordings exist, use the Python tone synthesis pattern to generate mood-matched demo tracks (bass drone + warm pad + arpeggio + soft percussion). See the reference file for the full 4-layer generation algorithm with mood-specific shaping.

#### Events / Live Shows (`events-section.tsx`)

Two-tier structure:
- Data source: `src/data/events.ts` — `Event[]` with id, title, date (ISO), venue, city, country, status (upcoming|past|cancelled), ticketUrl, ticketPrice, description, lineup, soldOut
- **Upcoming shows**: Cards with date block (day/month/year), vertical divider, venue/city/lineup/description, ticket CTA button (price or "TBA" or "Sold Out"). Sold shows get `opacity-60` treatment
- **Past shows**: Collapsible accordion (toggle button: "Show past shows (N)"). Past entries rendered simpler — date + title + venue
- Status badges per card: green "Upcoming", red "Sold Out", muted "Past"
- Empty state: "No shows scheduled yet" centered card

#### Merch Shop (`merch-section.tsx`)

WhatsApp-first catalog with no payment gateway:
- Data source: `src/data/merch.ts` — { id, name, price: { pyg, usd }, sizes[], color, description, waTemplate, image, category }
- Products grid (sm:2, lg:3 columns) with image placeholder (emoji per category + "Photo coming soon"), name, price, description (2-line clamp), size selector buttons, WhatsApp order button
- Category filter: "All", "Apparel", "Accessories", "Digital", "Posters" buttons — active state uses primary brand color
- Size selector: product.sizes mapped to small toggle buttons, first size selected by default
- WhatsApp order: builds template message from waTemplate, replaces [SIZE], opens wa.me link
- Categories in data file: array of { id, label } for expandability

#### Social Links / Connect (`contact-section.tsx` variant)

Dual-panel layout (md:grid-cols-2):
- Left: "Follow" panel — socials data array rendered as list items with SVG icon (per-platform inline SVG), name, handle, external link arrow
- Right: "Get in Touch" panel — WhatsApp card (green bg-tint with WA icon) + Email card (with mail icon)
- Social SVG icons stored inline in a `Record<string, ReactNode>` map (instagram, youtube, spotify, tiktok, x, soundcloud)
- Social data in `src/data/socials.ts`: exported `socials` array (for Follow panel) and `streamingLinks` array (for Music section)
- No markdown formatting — all JSX

### Navigation Pattern for 6+ Sections

When the nav bar has 5+ links on mobile, the layout breaks. Pattern used for Brahm:
- Desktop: horizontal links with gap-8, uppercase tracking-wider text
- Mobile: hamburger button toggles a full-width vertical menu below the header
- navLinks array: [{ label, href }] — shared between desktop nav map and mobile menu map
- Header gets `bg-[color]/90 backdrop-blur-md border-b` when scrolled past 50px (useState + scroll listener)

Band sites often have no `public/` directory. Remove the `COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public` line from Dockerfile to avoid build failure ("/app/public: not found").

## Auth System: Client → Real API Wiring

When upgrading from localStorage-only auth to real API-backed auth:

### SQLite Schema (must include sessions table)

The schema in `lib/db.ts` needs a `sessions` table for token-based auth:```
## Lead-to-Site Batch Deploy Script

See `scripts/deploy-lead-site.sh` — reusable script for deploying a single lead site to VPS. Run it individually per site (not in a loop) to avoid SSH timeout on long builds:

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
  token TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  expires_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
);
```

### Auth context patterns

1. **Always wrap children in AuthContext.Provider** — never conditionally render a fallback during loading/SSR. Children will try to call `useAuth()` and crash. Set all initial state to empty/null values.

2. **Guard localStorage** — check `typeof window !== "undefined"` before any `localStorage.getItem()` call. SSR runs this code.

3. **API helper with bearer token** — define a helper function that reads token from localStorage and attaches `Authorization: Bearer` header. This avoids repeating the pattern in every method.

4. **Auth routes pattern** — single POST /api/auth route with `action` field dispatching to login/register/me/logout branches. Rate limit with in-memory map keyed on `email:ip`.

5. **Addresses/Orders as separate routes** — PUT/DELETE on /api/addresses for CRUD, GET/POST on /api/orders. Authenticate by reading session token from `Authorization` header.

6. **Dynamic export** — all pages that use `useAuth` need `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"` to prevent Next.js 15 from trying to prerender them during build (will crash with "useAuth must be used within AuthProvider").

## Docker Build: Typescript devDep Problem

The base image `paragu-ai/next-base:node20` has `ENV NODE_ENV=production`. This causes `npm install` in the deps stage to skip devDependencies including `typescript`, which Next.js 15 needs to parse `next.config.ts`.

Fix: Set `ENV NODE_ENV=development` in the deps stage:

```dockerfile
# MANDATORY: next.config.ts must have output: "standalone"
# Without this, .next/standalone/server.js won't exist and container crashes on start
FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS deps
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=development
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install --legacy-peer-deps
```

Keep the builder stage at `NODE_ENV=production` (devDeps already installed by deps stage).

### Cloudflare SSL Mode for New Subdomains

New proxied (orange cloud) subdomains default to **Full (Strict)** mode in Cloudflare. This rejects self-signed origin certs with error 526.

Fix: In Cloudflare Dashboard → SSL/TLS → Overview, change from "Full (Strict)" to **"Full"** (accepts any origin cert). Or generate a Cloudflare Origin CA cert.

ACME HTTP-01 challenge does NOT work through Cloudflare proxy — Let's Encrypt hits CF edge IPs, not the VPS. Use DNS challenge or temporary DNS-only mode for ACME.

### Lead Site Productionizing (Auto-Generated Site Upgrade)

When deploying structurally-identical lead sites from a template (e.g., paragu-ai-leads), they often ship with:
- Only 3 pages (/, /contacto, /servicios)
- Missing HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 in Dockerfile → 504 errors

- No healthcheck, no non-root user
- No security headers, no robots/sitemap
- No about, FAQ, privacy, terms, 404, error pages

- Generic "Transformamos tu estilo" tagline copied across all business types

**Upgrade checklist for each lead site:** see `references/lead-site-deployment-checklist.md`. Pattern: same as productionizing any MVP site — add pages + Docker hardening + SEO + content localization.

## Common Pitfalls:

### Deep Business Research
Before building any client site, run the full research pipeline — see `references/deep-business-research.md`. This includes: scraping competitor sites for all images, mapping GPS coordinates, identifying related companies/subsidiaries, finding director/owner names via press interviews, and documenting cross-sell opportunities.

### Promo carousels: badge + title + description
- Blog category filtering: accented chars need NFD normalization before comparison
- Dynamic page titles: reset on unmount to prevent stale titles
- Social proof popups: position above WhatsApp float (bottom-32)
- Age gates: use timestamp-based expiry, not boolean localStorage flags
- Stats: initialize with "---" not "0" to avoid flash before animation
- Accessibility: add skip-to-content link with #main-content target
- Filter pagination: reset page to 1 when any filter changes
- Product images: SVG files in /public/images/ exist but es.json must reference them by correct path. "✦" placeholder means the image path is wrong or missing.
- Footer social dedup: if social bar (icon links) AND footer column (text links) both point to same Insta/WA, remove the text link column.
- Title template in layout.tsx shows default even with dynamic page titles -- client components set document.title in useEffect, but SSR output still shows the default until hydration.
- Price range sliders: two stacked range inputs overlap visually when values are close. Use a single range input (max price only) for simplicity.
- Anon review forms: must have `formRef.current?.reset()` + setRating state on submit. Interactive stars need `onClick` handler and conditional `text-warning`/`text-border` classes.
- Accented category names: URLs with accents don't match NFD-normalized comparisons. Always strip combining diacritics when generating URL slugs.
- Guest mode banner: use localStorage with 24h timestamp expiry, not sessionStorage (which shows on every new tab) or simple boolean (which never shows again).
- Page metadata/layout.tsx: the `title` export in metadata is SSR-only. Client-side dynamic titles need a separate `useEffect` with cleanup.
- SVG images on product cards: the SafeImage component renders `<img>` tags. Give the container `class="overflow-hidden"` so the SVG doesn't overflow the gradient background.
- Filter sidebar scroll position: when toggling filters on/off, the sidebar scrolls to top. Acceptable limitation unless building a custom scroll-position cache.
- **Mobile grids default to 1 column**: Tailwind's `grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2` without a base `grid-cols-2` class renders single-column on mobile (< 640px). Client prefered me to fix all product/card grids site-wide: `grid-cols-2 gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3`. Always set the base mobile column count explicitly — don't rely on `sm:` breakpoint for mobile users. Check ALL grids on the site when fixing this (homepage feat. products, features, testimonials, tienda, blog, blog category, envio, promociones, nosotros, skeleton).

### Roast Process (Post-Deploy Audit)

After any build or upgrade, run this systematic page-by-page audit. The full 18-point checklist from the Fun4Me session:

1. **Promo carousel**: badge + title + description rendered as one word? Badge needs `mr-1.5`, description needs `ml-2`, title needs proper whitespace.
2. **Blog content**: is the full article content rendering, or just the excerpt? If content lives in es.json, check it's long enough. If stored in a code map, verify all slugs have entries.
3. **Blog WhatsApp URL**: avoid literal template strings (`${encodeURIComponent(post.title)}` in href). Must use proper JS interpolation.
4. **Stats animation**: initial state is "0" or "—"? "0" causes flash of broken numbers. Use "—".
5. **Age gate**: boolean flag or timestamp? Timestamp with 7-day expiry is more resilient.
6. **Social proof popup**: does it overlap the WhatsApp float? Position at `bottom-32` or lower.
7. **Footer**: social platform links appear in both a column AND an icon bar? Remove the text-link column.
8. **Blog category URLs**: accented chars (Guías → guias)? Must NFD-normalize before filter comparison.
9. **Contact page**: has hours, address, multiple topic-specific buttons? Not just a single WhatsApp link.
10. **Privacy/Terms pages**: 8+ substantive sections, not 3-4 one-liners.
11. **Product images**: "✦" anywhere on product cards = image path broken or missing. SVGs should render.
12. **Page titles**: browser tab shows product name, not just site default. Check with `document.title` after hydration.
13. **Breadcrumbs**: visible on product/detail pages, not just hidden JSON-LD in `<head>`.
14. **Accessibility**: skip-to-content link present? `#main-content` target exists? Dark mode toggle has correct `aria-label`?
15. **Duplicate badges**: "Principiante" showing in both LevelBadge and SafetyBadges? Only one should show it.
16. **Price slider**: dual range inputs overlap visually? Replace with single max-price slider.
17. **Anon review form**: reset on submit? Stars are clickable? Form resets with `formRef.current?.reset()`.
18. **Guest mode banner**: shows every new tab (sessionStorage)? Use localStorage with 24h timestamp instead.

Discovered pattern from Fun4Me store audit. version: 1.1.0 tags: [audit, ecommerce, nextjs, supabase, bug-fixes, parallel-execution, batch-execution] triggers: - audit the website - analyze the gaps - find what's broken - make the store actually work - roast the site - 1000 items --- # E-Commerce Codebase Audit + Fix Pipeline ## When to Use - E-commerce site looks good but has hidden critical bugs - Need to find what's broken before real launch - Client asks "what's missing" or "make it better" - Post-MVP hardening before going live ## Phase 1: Parallel Audit (3 subagents) ### Subagent 1: Codebase Deep Audit Check every file for: 1. Broken imports / type mismatches 2. Placeholder/hardcoded data that should be dynamic 3. Stub pages (forms with no submit handler, no API calls) 4. Missing error handling 5. Missing loading states 6. Inconsistent constants (same value defined in 3 places) 7. Auth gaps (admin routes with no protection) 8. After EACH `execute_code` block that writes files: **git restore + rebuild to verify no file corruption** 4. Fix file corruption immediately with `git show HEAD: > ` 5. Re-apply changes with safer method (`patch` tool or `sed` via terminal) ### Critical: File Corruption from execute_code The Python sandbox's `read_file()` prepends line numbers to file content when the file was previously read with offset/limit pagination (which is common). Files written via `write_file()` after such a read contain the line numbers as actual file content. This causes: `"Unexpected token {. Expected . or ("` with line numbers at column 0. **Detection**: Build will fail. Check `head -5 ` — if it shows ` 1| 1|"use client"`, the file is corrupted. **Fix**: `git show HEAD: > ` **Prevention**: - For reading files to edit: use `terminal` with `cat ` or `git show HEAD:` - For writing new files: use `write_file` — this is safe - For editing existing files: use `patch` tool — but read the file with `read_file` FIRST using `terminal`, not execute_code - ALWAYS build after every execute_code block to catch corruption early ### Batch pattern: ``` bash heredoc 1: Fix business data (site.json + content/es.json) → build verify bash heredoc 2: SEO (sitemap, layout, structured data, headers) → build verify bash heredoc 3-6: UX + code + ecommerce + images → build verify bash heredoc 7: Mobile + security → build verify bash heredoc 8: Build + deploy + commit ``` Build check: `npm run build 2>&1 | tail -10`. 000-099: Data & Config - 100-199: SEO & Metadata - 200-299: UX & Design - 300-399: Content & Copy - 400-499: E-Commerce - 500-599: Images & Media - 600-699: Code Quality - 700-749: Mobile (50) - 750-799: Performance (50) - 800-849: Security (50) - 850-899: SEO Technical (50) - 900-949: Domain & DNS (50) - 950-999: Launch & Operations (50) ### Item format `- [ ] NNN Description` ### README summary Status emoji per area, biggest issue, top 20 quick wins (sorted by impact with effort), critical path. ## Phase 6: Client Handoff Questionnaire Generate a questionnaire with PRE-FILLED answers the client just edits: - Use the actual site data as defaults - Group by: Business Info, Brand, Products, Shipping, Payments, Legal, Social, Launch - Mark URGENT (blocks launch) vs nice-to-have - Pre-fill sensible defaults for shipping zones, return policy - Keep it short — 30-40 questions max, not more - Make it conversational, not a legal document This saves massive back-and-forth — client edits ~20% instead of writing 100%. ## Key Patterns & Lessons ### Batch via Python Heredocs ``` cd /repo/root python3 << 'PYEOF' import json, os with open("content/es.json") as f: c = json.load(f) for p in c["home"]["productCatalog"]["products"]: p["brand"] = get_brand(p["name"]) p["specs"] = get_specs(p["name"]) with open("content/es.json", "w") as f: json.dump(c, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) PYEOF ``` Advantages: no sandbox I/O issues, no timeout, full control. ### Image Bankruptcy Recovery MVPs often have: Unsplash URLs + empty imageUrl fields + no consistent style. Fix in one batch: generate placeholder SVGs (emoji + brand color per product), update ALL imageUrl fields, remove external dependencies. ### Cart Expansion (all in one batch) 1. Cart context + localStorage + hydration guard 2. Cart sidebar (slide-out, qty controls, line totals) 3. Product modal (image, description, qty selector) 4. Add-to-cart buttons (with disabled for out-of-stock) 5. Cart badge in header 6. Checkout message builder (WhatsApp, all items listed) 7. Shared phone config (not hardcoded per link) ### Build Error Pitfalls - **Escaped \n corruption**: Python `json.dump` with ensure_ascii=False writes literal `\n`, not newlines. Use heredocs. - **Sitemap dedup trap**: string.replace can leave duplicate declarations. Read file back after write. - **Tailwind v4 colors**: `bg-warning/10` may not work. Use explicit `bg-amber-500/10`. - **JSX tag mismatch**: copying `` → `` is a common bug. Verify matching tags. - **SVG elements in JSX**: `<>` fragments in conditional SVG rendering confuse the parser. ## Overview

For adult-oriented e-commerce sites that host events (parties, workshops, socials) and need:
- User accounts with saved profiles, addresses, and order history
- CI (Cédula de Identidad Paraguaya) upload + admin verification
- Event ticketing with QR entry and door-side verification
- Blacklist by CI number to block creeps from both tickets and toys

This pattern was developed for Fun4Me Store (fun4me.paragu-ai.com), Next.js 16 + Supabase + Docker Swarm.

---

## Architecture

```
User → Browser → Next.js 16 → Supabase
                                ├── Auth (email/password)
                                ├── PostgreSQL (profiles, orders, events, tickets, CI docs, blacklist)
                                └── Storage (CI images, receipts)
              → API Routes (order submission, blacklist check, CI verification)
              → Entry Verification page (mobile-first, QR + CI search)
```

**Three authentication tiers:**
1. **Guest** — browse, age gate (DOB cookie), add to cart, guest checkout
2. **Registered user** — saved addresses, order history, CI upload, toy purchase
3. **Verified user** — CI verified by admin, can buy tickets

---

## Tables (Supabase Schema)

### customers (extends existing `customers` table tied to `auth.users`)
```sql
-- Columns to add:
ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS email TEXT;
ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS date_of_birth DATE;
ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS avatar_url TEXT;
ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS preferences JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb;
ALTER TABLE customers ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS last_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ;
```

### customer_addresses
```sql
CREATE TABLE customer_addresses (
  id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  customer_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  label       TEXT DEFAULT 'Principal',
  full_name   TEXT NOT NULL,
  phone       TEXT,
  street      TEXT NOT NULL,
  city        TEXT NOT NULL,
  neighborhood TEXT,
  is_default  BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

### ci_documents
```sql
CREATE TABLE ci_documents (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  customer_id   UUID REFERENCES customers(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  ci_number     TEXT NOT NULL,
  full_name     TEXT NOT NULL,           -- Name as it appears on CI
  image_url     TEXT,                    -- CI photo/scan in Supabase Storage
  selfie_url    TEXT,                    -- Optional selfie holding CI
  verified      BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  verified_by   UUID,                    -- admin who verified
  verified_at   TIMESTAMPTZ,
  expires_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,             -- Verification expires after 1 year
  notes         TEXT,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

### events
```sql
CREATE TYPE event_status AS ENUM ('draft', 'published', 'cancelled', 'completed');

CREATE TABLE events (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  title           TEXT NOT NULL,
  slug            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  description     TEXT,
  short_desc      TEXT,

  end_date        TIMESTAMPTZ,              -- multi-day events
  venue           TEXT NOT NULL,
  venue_address   TEXT,
  city            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Asunción',
  image_url       TEXT,
  max_capacity    INTEGER NOT NULL,
  status          event_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft',

  organizer_name  TEXT DEFAULT 'Fun4Me Events',

);
```

### ticket_types
```sql
CREATE TABLE ticket_types (
  id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  event_id    UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES events(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  name        TEXT NOT NULL,                 -- "General", "VIP", "Early Bird"
  description TEXT,
  price       BIGINT NOT NULL CHECK (price >= 0),

  sold        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
```

```sql
  qr_code         TEXT UNIQUE,               -- unique QR code for entry
  status          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'valid'
                  CHECK (status IN ('valid', 'used', 'cancelled', 'refunded', 'transferred')),
  checked_in_at   TIMESTAMPTZ,
  checked_in_by   UUID,                      -- staff/agent who scanned
  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

### blacklist
```sql
CREATE TABLE blacklist (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  ci_number     TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  full_name     TEXT,
  reason        TEXT NOT NULL,
  evidence_url  TEXT,
  blocked_by    UUID,
  blocked_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  expires_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
  notes         TEXT
);
```

### blacklist_attempts (audit log)
```sql
CREATE TABLE blacklist_attempts (
  id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  ci_number   TEXT NOT NULL,
  customer_id UUID REFERENCES customers(id),
  action      TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'blocked_order', 'blocked_ticket'
  reason      TEXT,
  ip_address  TEXT,
  created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
```

---

## QR Code Format

```
ticket_id:event_id:holder_ci
```

Signed with HMAC-SHA256 using a server secret. Entry verification checks:
1. Decode HMAC (reject tampered QRs)
2. Look up ticket by ID
3. Verify status = 'valid'
4. Mark as 'used' + record checked_in_at + checked_in_by

Generate with `qrcode` npm package as SVG. Display on confirmation page with download option.

---

## Key Flows

### User flow: Buy ticket
1. Browse events → pick one → see ticket types + availability
2. Click "Comprar entradas"
3. If NOT logged in → prompt login/signup
4. If NOT CI-verified → prompt upload CI (image + number + name)
5. For each ticket enter: attendee name + CI number
6. Goes to checkout → choose payment (transfer/COD)
7. On payment: generate QR codes, save tickets, show confirmation page

### User flow: Enter event
1. Door staff opens `/admin/ingreso` on phone
2. Scans QR code (or types CI number)
3. System shows: ticket details, holder info, status
4. If valid → "Confirmar Ingreso" marks as used
5. If CI is blacklisted → red alert: "Persona NO autorizada"

### Admin flow: Blacklist CI
1. Go to `/admin/lista-negra`
2. Enter CI number + reason + optional evidence image
3. Save — CI is now blocked from ALL purchases
4. When blacklisted CI tries to buy → error message at checkout
5. When blacklisted CI tries to enter event → QR scanner shows red

### CI verification: CI is OPTIONAL for toy purchases, REQUIRED for tickets
- Toy checkout: optional CI field, used for age verification record
- Ticket checkout: enforced CI check before cart items can be added
- CI verification status: none → pending → approved → rejected (expires after 1 year)

---

## RLS Policies

### Public read
- Published events (status = 'published')
- Ticket types (all — need to show available types to purchase)
- Active coupons, active categories, active products

### Authenticated user
- Own profile (SELECT/UPDATE on customers)
- Own addresses (CRUD on customer_addresses)
- Own orders + order items (SELECT)
- Own CI documents (SELECT/INSERT)
- Own tickets (SELECT)
- Insert orders + order items (for checkout)

### Admin (service_role)
- Full CRUD on all tables
- Verified/unverified CI documents management
- Blacklist CRUD
- Verify/reject CI uploads

---

## CI Upload & Verification

### User side
- Upload front of CI card → stored in `ci-documents` Supabase Storage bucket (private)
- Auto-enter CI number (from image? current: manual)
- Submit for verification → status: 'pending'

### Admin side
- `/admin/verificaciones` — list of all submissions
- See image thumbnail, CI number, name, upload date
- Actions: Verify (approve) / Reject (with reason text)
- Verified CI shows green badge, rejected shows red with reason

### Storage bucket
```
ci-documents (private, 10MB max, jpeg/png/webp)
  └── {customer_id}/{timestamp}-front.jpg
  └── {customer_id}/{timestamp}-selfie.jpg
```

---

## Entry Verification Page (`/admin/ingreso/[event-slug]`)

Mobile-first, fullscreen, designed for door staff phones:
- QR code input field (or paste auto-detected)
- CI number search field (shows all tickets for that CI on this event)
- Result display:
  - Green: "Válido" → ticket type, holder name, event name
  - Red: "Ya usado" → checked_in_at timestamp, who checked them
  - Red: "Cancelado" → reason + admin who cancelled
  - Red: "CI NO AUTORIZADA" → blacklist alert
- Confirm button → marks as used + plays success beep
- Works offline (cache last sync)

---

## Dependency: User Account System

Before implementing ticketing, the site must have:
- Signup (`/registro`) with email + password + name + phone
- Login (`/iniciar-sesion`)
- Account dashboard (`/cuenta`) with tabs: Profile, Addresses, Orders, CI
- Auth context provider wrapping the app
- Supabase auth trigger that auto-creates customer row on signup
- Address CRUD (add/edit/delete/set default)
- Order history page (list past orders with status)

---

## Blacklist Enforcement Points

| Point | Check | Response |
|-------|-------|----------|
| Ticket purchase checkout | CI number against blacklist | Block order, show reason |
| Toy purchase with CI | If CI provided, check blacklist | Block order, show reason |
| Event entry | QR scan + CI search | Red alert + block entry |
| Admin manual check | CI search on /admin/lista-negra | Show status + history |

---

## Migration Order

1. `002_user_profiles.sql` — customer_addresses + customer columns
2. `003_ci_system.sql` — ci_documents + bucket + RLS
3. `004_ticketing.sql` — events + ticket_types + tickets + blacklist + blacklist_attempts + RLS

---

| /registro | Signup | No |

| /eventos | Event list | No |
| /eventos/[slug] | Event detail + buy tickets | No (auth on purchase) |
| /admin/verificaciones | CI verification queue | Admin |
| /admin/eventos | Manage events | Admin |
| /admin/eventos/nuevo | Create event | Admin |
| /admin/eventos/[id] | Edit event + ticket types | Admin |
| /admin/eventos/[id]/entradas | Manage tickets | Admin |
| /admin/eventos/[id]/asistentes | Attendee list | Admin |
| /admin/eventos/[id]/reporte | Attendance report | Admin |
| /admin/ingreso | Entry verification (all events) | Staff |
| /admin/ingreso/[event-slug] | Entry verification (one event) | Staff |
| /admin/lista-negra | Blacklist CRUD | Admin |
"}
